data.intent_description: Two serial interfaces experienced state changes: router2 Serial0/1/1 moved to oper=lower-layer-down while admin=up; router3 Serial0/1/1 is admin=down and oper=down. Goal: restore both links to oper=up (or confirm deliberate admin shutdown) and clear lower-layer fault on router2.

plan.problem: router2 Serial0/1/1 is oper=lower-layer-down with admin up; router3 Serial0/1/1 is administratively down causing oper down.
plan.fix_summary: Bring router3 Serial0/1/1 administratively up. Perform an admin bounce on router2 Serial0/1/1 to attempt to clear the lower-layer fault. These actions may cause a brief outage on the serial links.

messages.content:
What was observed
- router2 Serial0/1/1: admin=up, oper=lower-layer-down.
- router3 Serial0/1/1: admin=down, oper=down.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- router2: transient lower-layer fault on the serial link.
- router3: administratively shut down.

Changes made
- router3: attempted "no shutdown" — interface was already enabled.
- router2: performed an admin bounce (shutdown then no shutdown) to clear the lower-layer condition.

Verification
- Passed. Evidence:
  - router2 Serial0/1/1 now admin=up oper=up (speed=2048000, last_change=2026-05-27T15:24:46; source get_interface_status call_4bIAIcu…).
  - router3 Serial0/1/1 now admin=up oper=up (speed=2064384, last_change=2026-05-27T14:42:47; source get_interface_status call_0zCj5R19…).
  - Network DB updated to reflect both interfaces admin=up oper=up.
- Remaining issues: none.

  - router3 Serial0/1/1 now admin=up oper=up (speed=2064384, last_change=2026-05-27T14:42:47; source get_interface_status call_0zCj5R19…).
